message:
feat(core): implement proper Connection::close() and StorageCursor-only path

This commit makes two significant architectural improvements to the
database connection and cursor subsystems:

1. Connection Lifecycle Management (bd-3bsn follow-up):
   - Added explicit Connection::close() method for clean shutdown
   - Implements proper pager/WAL shutdown sequence:
     a) Roll back any active transaction
     b) Run passive checkpoint (WAL -> DB)
     c) Mark connection closed to prevent double-cleanup in Drop
   - Added `closed: RefCell<bool>` field to guard idempotent shutdown
   - close_internal() provides best-effort mode for Drop impl
   - Views are now cleared on state reload since view persistence from
     sqlite_master (type='view') is not yet implemented

2. StorageCursor-Only Execution (bd-1xrs):
   - Removed MemCursor fallback from OpenRead/OpenWrite opcodes
   - StorageCursor is now the ONLY cursor path - open_storage_cursor
     must succeed or the operation fails with a clear error message
   - This simplifies the execution model and removes dead code paths
   - Guarantees all data access goes through the B-tree layer

3. Seek Opcode B-tree Integration (bd-3pti):
   - Rewrote SeekGE/SeekGT/SeekLE/SeekLT to properly use table_move_to
   - SeekGE: Position at first row >= key
   - SeekGT: Position at first row > key (advance if exact match)
   - SeekLE: Position at last row <= key (use prev if past key)
   - SeekLT: Position at last row < key
   - Proper EOF handling for all seek variants

4. Minor cleanup:
   - Fixed duplicate doc comment in compat_persist.rs parse_columns_from_create_sql
   - Updated beads issue tracker state

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
